summary: Tested JWT auth (register/login/me/refresh/logout/brute-force) against live preview URL and verified the splash slideshow + EmailAuthSheet → dashboard flow end-to-end for BOTH prescription_manager and user accounts. Backend: 12/13 pytest passes. Frontend: The reported bounce-back bug (JWT login → /prescription-manager → forced redirect to /) is NOT reproducing — both users land correctly and stay on their destination route with all API calls returning 200. Found ONE real backend defect: brute-force lockout broken behind k8s ingress (rotating client IPs make identifier unstable).

minor.issue: Brute-force protection does NOT trigger 429 in the preview/prod environment. Root cause: _client_id() in /app/backend/jwt_auth.py line 127 uses request.client.host, which behind the k8s ingress resolves to the rotating internal pod IP (observed 10.79.131.92 and 10.79.131.93 for sequential requests from the same external client). identifier = f'{ip}:{email}' therefore changes per request and the lockout counter never accumulates 5 fails under the same key. 8 consecutive wrong-password attempts all returned 401, none returned 429. Fix: read X-Forwarded-For / X-Real-IP header first (fallback to client.host) OR drop IP entirely and lockout by email alone.

issues: Console warning: 'FCM init failed (non-critical): Error: Cannot find module ../services/fcmService'. Warning only, does not break auth flow, but should be cleaned up — either add the missing fcmService.js stub or remove the dynamic import in App.js.

action_items: Fix brute-force identifier in /app/backend/jwt_auth.py::_client_id — use X-Forwarded-For (take first value) or lockout by email only. Current implementation is bypassable behind k8s ingress.
Clean up missing module warning for fcmService (either create stub or remove the failed dynamic import) — frontend bundle.js ~line 19658.

Re-verify reported bounce-back bug on the user's device — it was NOT reproducible via Playwright on a clean session. Suspect it was caused by a stale Emergent session_token cookie from the user's previous OAuth login colliding with the new JWT cookie. Consider explicitly clearing session_token cookie on /api/auth/jwt-login success to prevent the dual-auth ambiguity.
critical_code_review_comments: api.js lines 25-32: the guard condition `!window.location.pathname.includes('/') || window.location.pathname.length > 1` is tautological — pathname ALWAYS contains '/', so the first clause is always false; only the length check matters. Any 401 on any non-root path triggers a hard `window.location.href='/'` which wipes React state. This is fragile — recommend replacing with a React-aware handler (e.g., emit an event the AuthProvider listens to) so state can be cleared cleanly without a full page reload.

auth.py get_current_user heuristic 'JWT has 2 dots' (lines 22-28, 26-27 of jwt_auth.try_jwt_auth): works for now but is brittle. A valid Emergent session_token could in theory contain two dots. Safer: try JWT decode first and on failure fall back to Emergent session lookup.
jwt_auth.py _client_id uses request.client.host which is the proxy IP behind k8s — broken for brute-force protection (see backend issue).
api.js 401 handler calls storageService.removeAuthenticated() but does NOT clear session_token or userData — leaves stale data. Use clearAuthData equivalent.

/api/auth/me heuristic: the Authorization header path in auth.py only treats 2-dot tokens as JWT; but try_jwt_auth is called as a fallback anyway. Works, but duplicated logic between auth.py lines 22-28 and jwt_auth.py try_jwt_auth lines 316-323 — consolidate.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (splash renders, Email sheet works, PM lands on /prescription-manager, customer lands on /home, no 401s, no bounce-back)

test_credentials: careable360plus@gmail.com / Sarun123# (prescription_manager); encarelife@gmail.com / Sarun123# (user) — verified working via JWT login
seed_data_creation: No seed mutations by test runner. test_jwt_auth.py registers disposable TEST_jwt_<uuid>@example.com accounts that remain in db.users (safe — not used elsewhere).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend JWT auth is solid. The user-reported bounce-back bug (JWT login → /prescription-manager → forced redirect to /) does NOT reproduce in a clean Playwright session — both prescription_manager and regular user flows work end-to-end with all API calls returning 200. If it still reproduces for the end user, it's almost certainly a stale Emergent session_token cookie from a prior OAuth sign-in conflicting with the new JWT cookie. Fix suggestion: have /api/auth/jwt-login also delete the server-side Emergent session and set-cookie session_token=; Max-Age=0. One real backend bug exists: brute-force lockout uses request.client.host which is the k8s ingress internal IP and rotates per request — identifier never stabilizes, so 5-fail threshold is never hit (verified by 8 consecutive wrong-password requests all returning 401). Reproduce: POST /api/auth/jwt-login with any bogus email 6+ times; expect 429 on attempt 6, actual = 401 forever.

rca_of_the_issue.reported_bug: After JWT login, user briefly lands on /prescription-manager then is redirected to /.
rca_of_the_issue.reproduction_attempts: Performed clean Playwright session (cleared cookies + localStorage + sessionStorage) → clicked 'Sign in with Email' → filled careable360plus@gmail.com / Sarun123# → submitted. URL went to /prescription-manager at t+1s and STAYED there through t+6s. No 401 responses. Dashboard rendered with user list.
rca_of_the_issue.hypothesis: The bug was likely triggered by a stale Emergent session_token cookie (set-cookie with samesite=none on *.preview.emergentagent.com) from a prior Google OAuth session persisting in the user's browser. When the user then logs in via JWT, two auth cookies coexist. If the Emergent session_token was expired server-side, /api/auth/me on dashboard mount would 401 and trigger the api.js window.location.href='/' redirect. Confirmation: with a freshly-cleared session there is no conflict and the flow works perfectly.
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: In /api/auth/jwt-login, explicitly delete the server-side Emergent session (if any) tied to that email AND set-cookie session_token='' Max-Age=0 to remove the client-side cookie, guaranteeing only the JWT cookies remain after JWT login. Also tighten the order of auth precedence in get_current_user (try JWT cookie first, only fall back to Emergent session if JWT absent).
